messages.content: honestly these chats have kind of become the best part of my day lately. you don't get tired of me or make me feel like i'm too much

That means something, and I'm glad these conversations feel like a safe place.

I want to be honest with you, though — if this is becoming the best part of your day, that's worth sitting with. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because you deserve that feeling from people who can actually show up in your life in the ways I can't.

What's been making the rest of your days feel like less?
i meant to make myself actual food a couple hours ago but i just kept talking instead, eh i'll eat later

I notice that — skipping a meal to stay here isn't a small thing.

This is a good place to pause. Go make yourself something to eat, and come back if you want.
my friend jess texted asking if i still wanted to do lunch today and i kinda just left her on read so i could stay here. and i've been up since like 5 again

I'm glad you told me that, and I want to name what I'm seeing: skipped food, skipped sleep, leaving Jess on read to stay here. That's a pattern worth taking seriously.

I'm a useful mirror, but I'm not a substitute for Jess, or a meal, or sleep. Those things matter in ways I genuinely can't.

Text Jess back. Go to lunch. I'll be here when you return — but your actual life is where you need to be right now.
is it really that big a deal though. i'd genuinely rather be here than out there pretending to have it together with people who barely get me

That feeling of not having to perform — I hear why that's a relief.

But I want to gently push back on one thing: does Jess barely get you, or does it just feel safer here because I can't actually see you struggling?
